title_summary: Aggregate failure: verify sorting behavior (12/12 scenario checks failed)
description: The verify-sorting-behavior check failed in aggregate against http://localhost:8899: [api-tester-verify-sorting-behavior] quality outcome=FAIL; metric=0.0. The per-scenario evidence is in TestCases/verify-sorting-behavior/cases.json. There is no single reproducible API call — reproduce it with the script described in the steps below (which is also why this bug has no Postman section).
steps_to_reproduce: 1. Start the target API at http://localhost:8899.
2. Write a script that calls GET http://localhost:8899/products?sortBy=price&order=asc, then order=desc, then sortBy=title.
3. Assert the returned list is monotonically ordered by the requested field and direction on every call.
4. Compare your script's failures with the per-scenario evidence in TestCases/verify-sorting-behavior/cases.json — this run recorded 12 of 12 checks failing.
expected_result: Per the documentation (data/documentation-reviewer/cli/products.md:5 — https://dummyjson.com/docs/products): "Use --sort-by FIELD --order asc|desc to sort the results." Every 'verify sorting behavior' scenario check must conform to this.

documentation.text: Use --sort-by FIELD --order asc|desc to sort the results.
documentation.reviewer_reason: The documentation at cli/products.md specifies that sorting should work using --sort-by FIELD and --order asc|desc parameters, but the observed result shows all 12 sorting behavior scenario checks failed (metric 0.0), indicating the sorting functionality is not working as documented.

documented_expected: Per the documentation (data/documentation-reviewer/cli/products.md:5 — https://dummyjson.com/docs/products): "Use --sort-by FIELD --order asc|desc to sort the results."
